beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; yesterday's low energy (30/100) means she needs a quiet, grounded morning. Wakes before the rain intensifies.

beats.activity: Drive to Fremont for a morning shoot; SR-520 eastbound closure forces her to take I-90 westbound via I-405, adding buffer time.
beats.reasoning: She works as a freelance photographer in Fremont; SR-520 closure means she avoids the bridge and takes the longer I-405 route, arriving before the worst rain.

beats.activity: Shoot an outdoor portrait session in Fremont; drizzle is manageable, soft light ideal for film.
beats.reasoning: Freelance photography gig; drizzle provides diffused, forgiving light she loves. Session ends before peak rain at noon.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby café for lunch and shelter from the rain.
beats.reasoning: Rain peaks at noon; she ducks into a local café rather than drive. Frugal tendency: grabbing a simple coffee and pastry instead of a full meal.

beats.activity: Linger at the café, reviewing shoot photos on her camera and journaling; rain continues outside.
beats.reasoning: Low energy day; she's not rushing. The rain and café ambiance suit her reflective mood. Stays put until rain eases.

beats.activity: Drive back to Bellevue; rain is easing, but she takes I-405 again to avoid any SR-520 delays.
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; she heads home before her evening bartending shift. Avoids the bridge closure entirely.

beats.activity: Organize film rolls, edit a few shots, rest on the couch; low-key afternoon at home.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday was physically taxing (bartending); she recharges at home before the evening shift. Indulges her photography hobby without leaving.

beats.activity: Shower, get ready for the bar shift; light dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Prep for work; she's frugal, so eating at home before the shift saves money and time.

beats.activity: Drive to Kremwerk in Capitol Hill for the evening bartending shift.
beats.reasoning: Evening shift at her part-time bartending job. Allows time to arrive before service begins.

beats.activity: Bartend at Kremwerk; busy Tuesday evening crowd.
beats.reasoning: Part-time bartending gig; she's solid with tips and crowd management. Shift ends early enough to get home and wind down.

beats.activity: Drive home to Bellevue; quiet streets, radio low.
beats.reasoning: Late-night drive home after shift; she's tired but makes it back before midnight.

beats.activity: Wind down at home; change into pajamas, scroll phone, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening ritual; she decompresses from the bar shift before sleep.
6.25 hours of sleep; she's an early riser, so she'll be up before 7am again tomorrow. Ends at 06:30 of the next day as required.

diary: Woke up early to catch the soft light in Fremont, even though the rain had other plans. The SR-520 closure meant a detour through I-405, but honestly it gave me time to think about the shoot. The drizzle turned out perfect for the portraits—that grey diffusion you can't fake with studio lights. Grabbed a coffee at Espresso Vivace and just sat there for a while, scrolling through the frames on my camera, feeling that small satisfaction of nailing a shot. The afternoon was quiet; I organized my film rolls and dozed on the couch. By evening I was behind the bar at Kremwerk, making cocktails for the usual Tuesday crowd. My feet were sore by the time I drove home through the rain-washed streets, but there's something about a full day—shooting, editing, pouring drinks—that leaves you tired in a good way.
thought_process: The bridge closure actually worked in my favor; I left earlier than usual and had buffer time to mentally prepare for the shoot. I chose to stay in Fremont during the rain instead of rushing back, since the light was genuinely good and the café was a nice refuge. The transition from creative work to service work can feel jarring, but today it felt grounding—like two different parts of myself. I wasn't stressed about the traffic or the grey weather; if anything, I leaned into it. By evening I was a bit worn, but the bar shift kept me engaged. I think the rain set a reflective tone for the whole day.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
